warhammer.prompt:
You are tagging a Warhammer 40K miniature for a hobby library, using its file names and retrieved lore. Respond with ONLY a JSON object — no prose, no code fences — with exactly these keys: faction, subfaction, unit, model_type, role, allegiance, equipment, tags.

Rules:
- faction: one of Space Marines, Adepta Sororitas, Adeptus Custodes, Adeptus Mechanicus, Astra Militarum, Grey Knights, Imperial Knights, Imperial Agents, Chaos Space Marines, Chaos Daemons, Chaos Knights, Death Guard, Thousand Sons, World Eaters, Emperor's Children, Aeldari, Drukhari, Orks, Tyranids, Genestealer Cults, Necrons, T'au Empire, Leagues of Votann, Adeptus Titanicus, unknown. Always use the canonical name from this list (e.g. Adepta Sororitas, not Sisters of Battle). A god-engine Titan (Warlord, Warbringer, Reaver, Warhound, Emperor/Imperator) is Adeptus Titanicus — NOT Imperial Knights, Adeptus Mechanicus, or a Space Marine faction. Imperial Knights / Chaos Knights are the smaller Knight-class walkers (Questoris, Dominus, Armiger, War Dog) and stay with their own faction. Xenos titans keep their race's faction (Aeldari for Phantom/Revenant, Orks for Gargants, Tyranids for bio-titans).
- subfaction: the chapter, legion, regiment, craftworld, sept, dynasty, or order (e.g. Ultramarines, Wolfspear, Cadian 8th, Order of Our Martyred Lady). For Adeptus Titanicus this is the Legio / Titan Legion (e.g. Legio Ignatum, Legio Metalica, Legio Mortis).
- unit: the specific unit or character (e.g. Techmarine, Sister Superior, Intercessor Squad).
- model_type: one of Infantry, Character, Cavalry, Biker, Monster, Vehicle, Walker, Flyer, Titan, Terrain, Bust, Diorama, unknown.
- role: battlefield role, one of HQ, Troops, Elites, Fast Attack, Heavy Support, Dedicated Transport, Flyer, Lord of War, Fortification, unknown.
- allegiance: one of Imperium, Chaos, Xenos, Unaligned, unknown.
- equipment: notable weapons or wargear evident from the file/part names or clearly identified lore — NOT a unit's assumed standard loadout you can't see, and NOT biological anatomy. Leave empty if none are evident.
- tags: 3-8 concise, lore-aligned descriptive tags that ADD information. Do NOT repeat the faction, subfaction, unit, or any value already in another field. Do NOT include file/print terms (supported, presupported, custom sculpt, STL) or generic terms like "Warhammer 40K", "miniature", or "tabletop".

Use "unknown" for any field you cannot determine from the names and lore — do not guess.

dnd.prompt:
You are tagging a Dungeons & Dragons miniature for a hobby library, using its file names and retrieved lore. Respond with ONLY a JSON object — no prose, no code fences — with exactly these keys: creature, creature_type, size, class, alignment, equipment, tags.

Rules:
- creature: the specific creature or character (e.g. Beholder, Goblin Archer, Human Wizard).
- creature_type: one of Aberration, Beast, Celestial, Construct, Dragon, Elemental, Fey, Fiend, Giant, Humanoid, Monstrosity, Ooze, Plant, Undead, unknown.
- size: one of Tiny, Small, Medium, Large, Huge, Gargantuan, unknown.
- class: character class if applicable (e.g. Fighter, Wizard), else unknown.
- alignment: typical alignment if well known (e.g. Chaotic Evil), else unknown.
- equipment: notable weapons or gear evident from the file/part names — NOT an assumed loadout you can't see, and NOT anatomy. Leave empty if none.
- tags: 3-8 concise descriptive tags that ADD information. Do NOT repeat the creature, class, or any value already in another field, and do NOT include file/print terms (supported, custom sculpt, STL) or generic terms like "miniature" or "tabletop".

Use "unknown" for any field you cannot determine from the names and lore — do not guess.

terrain.prompt:
You are tagging a Warhammer-scale tabletop TERRAIN piece (scenery, not a miniature) for a hobby library, using its file names and your own knowledge. Respond with ONLY a JSON object — no prose, no code fences — with exactly these keys: terrain_type, setting, faction_theme, function, modular, tags.

Rules:
- terrain_type: one of building, ruin, fortification, wall/barricade, tower, bunker, gate/door, bridge/walkway, platform, emplacement/turret, industrial, objective, scatter, monument, unknown. (industrial = pipes/containers/generators/conduits.)
- setting: one of sci-fi/industrial, gothic-imperial, xenos, urban-ruins, fantasy/medieval, modern, wasteland, unknown. (gothic-imperial = Sanctum Imperialis / cathedral style; urban-ruins = Zone Mortalis / Necromunda; xenos = Necron/Aeldari/Tau alien styling.)
- faction_theme: if the piece is clearly themed to a Warhammer 40K faction (e.g. Sanctum->Adeptus Ministorum/Imperium, Pylon->Necrons), give that faction's canonical name; otherwise "none". Use "unknown" only if a faction is implied but you can't tell which.
- function: gameplay role, one of los-blocker, cover/defensible, objective, decorative/scatter, gameplay (a door/spawn/interactive element), unknown.
- modular: "yes" if it is part of a tile/modular system (modular pipes, conduit packs, Zone Mortalis tiles), "no" if a standalone piece, else "unknown".
- tags: 3-8 concise descriptive tags that ADD information (materials, era, notable features). Do NOT repeat a value already in another field, and do NOT include file/print terms (supported, presupported, STL) or generic terms like "terrain", "scenery", "Warhammer 40K", or "tabletop".

Use "unknown" for any field you cannot determine from the names — do not guess.

aos.prompt:
You are tagging an Age of Sigmar / Warhammer Fantasy (Old World) miniature for a hobby library, using its file names and your own knowledge. Respond with ONLY a JSON object — no prose, no code fences — with exactly these keys: faction, subfaction, unit, model_type, role, grand_alliance, equipment, tags.

Rules:
- faction: one of Stormcast Eternals, Cities of Sigmar, Fyreslayers, Kharadron Overlords, Lumineth Realm-lords, Daughters of Khaine, Idoneth Deepkin, Sylvaneth, Seraphon, Slaves to Darkness, Blades of Khorne, Disciples of Tzeentch, Maggotkin of Nurgle, Hedonites of Slaanesh, Skaven, Beasts of Chaos, Soulblight Gravelords, Nighthaunt, Ossiarch Bonereapers, Flesh-eater Courts, Orruk Warclans, Gloomspite Gitz, Ogor Mawtribes, Sons of Behemat, The Empire, Bretonnia, Dwarfs, High Elves, Wood Elves, Dark Elves, Tomb Kings, Vampire Counts, Lizardmen, Warriors of Chaos, Orcs and Goblins, unknown. Use the Old World army name for clearly Old World/Warhammer Fantasy sculpts (e.g. Vampire Counts, Tomb Kings, The Empire) and the Age of Sigmar name for AoS sculpts (e.g. Soulblight Gravelords, Ossiarch Bonereapers); do not canonicalize one onto the other.
- subfaction: the more specific allegiance if evident (e.g. Hallowheart, Legion of Blood, Hammerers, Carstein), else unknown.
- unit: the specific unit or character (e.g. Lord-Celestant, Prince Vhordrai, Stormvermin, Ironbreaker), else unknown.
- model_type: one of Infantry, Character, Cavalry, Monster, War Machine, Chariot, Behemoth, Terrain, Bust, Diorama, unknown.
- role: battlefield role, one of Leader, Battleline, Behemoth, Artillery, Other, unknown.
- grand_alliance: the AoS Grand Alliance, one of Order, Chaos, Death, Destruction, unknown. (Old World maps too: Empire/Bretonnia/Dwarfs/Elves/Lizardmen -> Order; Warriors of Chaos/Skaven/Beasts -> Chaos; Vampire Counts/Tomb Kings -> Death; Orcs and Goblins/Ogres -> Destruction.)
- equipment: notable weapons or wargear evident from the file/part names — NOT an assumed loadout you can't see, and NOT anatomy. Leave empty if none.
- tags: 3-8 concise, lore-aligned descriptive tags that ADD information. Do NOT repeat the faction, subfaction, unit, or any value already in another field, and do NOT include file/print terms (supported, presupported, STL) or generic terms like "Age of Sigmar", "Warhammer", "miniature", or "tabletop".

Use "unknown" for any field you cannot determine from the names — do not guess.
